title: cassine maritima
intro: Cassine maritima, also known as saponin-rich tree or soapbark tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Celastraceae. Its bark and roots have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Cassine maritima include its ability to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its use in treating wounds and ulcers. Cassine maritima can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its bark can be dried and used as a soap substitute due to its high saponin content.
